<findings>

[observation] [info] Cohesion of 0.75 is solid for a 3-file zone; the test file, hook, and utility form a tight triangle with no extraneous files.
[observation] [info] Zone has no entry points defined; if the hook is the intended public surface, marking it as an entry point would make the API contract explicit to tooling.
[relationship] [info] dom-performance-monitoring → web-viewer is the only cross-zone edge for this zone; if performance monitoring ever needs to instrument zones other than web-viewer (e.g. web-server), a shared utility layer would be required to avoid duplicating measurement logic.
[anti-pattern] [info] With only 2 production files (hook + utility), this zone is below the minimum viable zone size for independent lifecycle management; if no additional performance-monitoring files are planned, merging these two files into the web-viewer zone as an internal module would reduce zone overhead without losing the clean separation of concerns.
[suggestion] [info] Document the exception: if keeping the hook outside web-viewer is intentional, add a comment in the hook file or a zone README noting that performance-monitoring hooks are isolated here by design. Without this, future contributors will not know whether the placement is a deliberate pattern or an oversight.
[suggestion] [info] Harmonize naming form: rename the zone to 'dom-performance-monitor' (noun) to match the actual filenames (dom-performance-monitor.ts, use-dom-performance-monitor.ts), or rename the files to use the gerund form — but resolve the noun/gerund split so zone ID, zone name, and filenames share one convention.

</findings>

<insights>

- The utility/hook split is clean: dom-performance-monitor.ts owns measurement logic, use-dom-performance-monitor.ts owns React lifecycle — good separation of concerns within a small zone.
- Low coupling (0.25) keeps this zone independently testable and replaceable if the performance strategy changes.
- The single inbound import from web-viewer confirms this zone is consumed correctly as a focused leaf module rather than a shared utility.
- Cohesion of 0.75 is solid for a 3-file zone; the test file, hook, and utility form a tight triangle with no extraneous files.
- Zone has no entry points defined; if the hook is the intended public surface, marking it as an entry point would make the API contract explicit to tooling.
- As one of two 'satellite' zones in this batch alongside orchestrator-e2e-tests, dom-performance-monitoring's single outbound edge (dom → web-viewer) and zero inbound edges from non-web-viewer zones confirm it is a true leaf with no hidden fans-out — changes here have a blast radius of exactly one zone.
- dom-performance-monitoring → web-viewer is the only cross-zone edge for this zone; if performance monitoring ever needs to instrument zones other than web-viewer (e.g. web-server), a shared utility layer would be required to avoid duplicating measurement logic.
- The zone's cohesion score (0.75) is partially inflated by the test file being one of only three zone members — stripping test files from the membership calculation would give a truer cohesion picture of the hook+utility pair alone; a two-file production zone would likely score lower and warrant a merge evaluation.
- With only 2 production files (hook + utility), this zone is below the minimum viable zone size for independent lifecycle management; if no additional performance-monitoring files are planned, merging these two files into the web-viewer zone as an internal module would reduce zone overhead without losing the clean separation of concerns.
- The zone's abbreviated identifier 'dom' (visible in cross-zone import labels as 'dom → web-viewer') does not match its full name 'dom-performance-monitoring', creating an ambiguous short-form that could be confused with DOM APIs or the browser DOM layer by readers unfamiliar with the zone graph.
- The production files use 'monitor' (noun: dom-performance-monitor.ts, use-dom-performance-monitor.ts) while the zone name uses 'monitoring' (gerund: dom-performance-monitoring) — a subtle naming form mismatch that should be harmonized to one convention across zone ID, zone name, and filenames.
- This zone is the only location in the monorepo where a React hook lives outside the web-viewer zone; all other hooks are co-located in web-viewer. This outlier placement is not inherently wrong but creates an implicit exception to an otherwise consistent convention that is nowhere documented.
- Harmonize naming form: rename the zone to 'dom-performance-monitor' (noun) to match the actual filenames (dom-performance-monitor.ts, use-dom-performance-monitor.ts), or rename the files to use the gerund form — but resolve the noun/gerund split so zone ID, zone name, and filenames share one convention.
- Document the exception: if keeping the hook outside web-viewer is intentional, add a comment in the hook file or a zone README noting that performance-monitoring hooks are isolated here by design. Without this, future contributors will not know whether the placement is a deliberate pattern or an oversight.
- [call graph] 248 internal calls, 1 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
